summary: Phase I — Digital Twin token-based Report Approval flow (no login). Full backend + frontend testing. 14/14 pytest cases PASS and 6/6 Playwright UI flows PASS. Token issuance, JWT structure, public info endpoint (200/400/410/wrong-type/tampered-signature), decide endpoint (confirmed, needs_changes, single-use 409, pydantic 422, malformed 400), sender in-app notification — all verified. Frontend: form load, decision picker rings (emerald/amber), comment fill, submit → success, reload → already-decided, invalid token → error state, URL preset ?decision=confirmed → pre-selected button — all verified end-to-end on the public URL.

issues: Cosmetic only: when token is invalid, the page briefly fires 2 background 401s to /api/auth/me (likely a global axios interceptor or shared header probing auth). The page itself works fine (purely token-based), but these console errors are noisy on a deliberately-public route. Consider gating auth/me on `localStorage.getItem('token')` or excluding /report-respond/* from the global axios bootstrap.

action_items: (Optional polish) Avoid the two /api/auth/me 401s on the public /report-respond/:token route — gate the global auth bootstrap on localStorage token presence or skip it for this route. Functionality is unaffected.
critical_code_review_comments: digital_twin.py — _decode_report_approval_token correctly distinguishes ExpiredSignature → 410 vs everything else → 400, and validates payload type ('dt_report_approval'). Good.

decide endpoint correctly uses MongoDB array_filters for atomic single-history-entry update; second POST returns 409 because the filter no longer matches once approval_status != 'pending'. Verified via tests.
JWT exp is set ~30 days; tests confirmed 28–31 day window.
Pydantic decision pattern correctly returns 422 for values other than 'confirmed'/'needs_changes'.

ReportApprovalPage.jsx is self-contained (255 lines, well under 700-line threshold), all interactive elements have data-testid, axios calls use process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, loading/error/success states properly handled.
Token in URL path (not query) is good — won't leak via Referer header on outbound links inside the page.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase_i_approval.py (new — 14 tests covering issuance, JWT decode, info endpoint, decide endpoint, sender notification, tamper/wrong-type/expired token paths)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@propmanage.io / Admin123!  |  Specialist: specialist@propmanage.io / Spec123! (DT Pro grant auto-applied in fixtures)
seed_data_creation: Each pytest module creates an isolated project TEST_PhaseI_Approval with one pin and deletes it on teardown. Frontend Playwright run created project TEST_FE_Approval which was deleted post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-use /app/backend/tests/test_phase_i_approval.py for regression. JWT_SECRET is currently hardcoded in the test (matches backend/.env value 24bcea8b...db2). If the secret is rotated, update the constant or load it from backend/.env. Email delivery is not asserted (only in-app notification is checked) because mailbox access is not available in CI; the email path is exercised by the code under test (sender notification block in decide endpoint), so if the in-app notification works, the email send code was reached.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found. Feature works end-to-end on production-equivalent preview URL.
